code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test13-MAR-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. HbA1c is low, which may reflect anemia or recent hypoglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent clinical evaluation for renal pathology and metabolic disturbances.
